criteria.text: A new segment is created once the current one exceeds a configured size
covered_by: TestSegmentWriter (asserts >=3 segment files exist for a forced-rotation workload, and no segment exceeds maxSegmentBytes)

criteria.text: No record is ever split across segments
covered_by: TestSegmentWriter (each segment parses cleanly end-to-end via ReadSegment with no truncation error; concatenated payload order matches append order)

criteria.text: go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestSegmentWriter -race: write enough records to force rotation, assert segment boundaries and record integrity
Oversized single record (bigger than maxSegmentBytes) hard-errors rather than being split/truncated (repo idiom from btree/node.go)
OpenWriter on a pre-existing WAL dir resumes at the correct next segment number without clobbering prior segments
